name: Guadramilese

ancestors.notes: Medieval Asturian-Leonese of Portugal: the common ancestor of Guadramilese, Rionorese, and Mirandese.

Rionorese sister dialect. Guadramil and Rio de Onor are 5 km apart; the two varieties share ~85% of their phonological rules and diverge principally in morphological paradigms (article system, imperfect 3pl suffix, imperfect of ir) and in a handful of sibilant and vowel realizations.
Sanabrian Leonese (Zamora province), geographically adjacent across the border. Shared features include betacism and Оё-merger.
Old Portuguese adstrate: intensifying contact pressure from the 15th century onward drove Portuguesification of the article system and lexical replacement.

notes:
Guadramilese (guadramilГ©s). SUB-ZONE DIPHTHONG NOTE (from FrГ­as-Conde, El Habla de Sanabria, p. 3): The Guadramil sub-zone (Portuguese side) shows more extreme /wo/ diphthongization than the Spanish-side Sanabrian varieties: *cuorpo* (corpo), *cuarda* (corda), *muarto* (morto). This is the most extreme realization of ЕЋ > /wo/ in the zone. Encoded via the graphemes_base inheritance from ast-PT-x-rionor (вџЁuГґвџ©=/wЙ”/, вџЁuГўвџ©=/wЙђ/). No dedicated Guadramil monograph exists; the sub-zone data is from the comparative table in the Sanabrese source.
  ORIGINAL NOTES: Guadramilese (guadramilГ©s), the nearly-extinct Asturleonese variety spoken in Guadramil, municipality of Vinhais, BraganГ§a district, Portugal вЂ” approximately 5 km south of Rio de Onor along the Portuguese-Spanish border. Fewer than 50 speakers as of the early 2000s. No dedicated linguistic monograph exists. Primary data source: Miro (2026) Paper III ('One Recording, Zero Grammar'), which analyses a ~400-word transcript of two elderly speakers describing traditional wedding customs. Supporting sources: Carvalho (1984) comparative notes; Santos (1967) 'falares fronteiriГ§os de TrГЎs-os-Montes'. PHONOLOGICAL PROFILE: Guadramilese is phonologically near-identical to Rionorese вЂ” all grapheme-to-IPA rules are inherited via graphemes_base from ast-PT-x-rionor. The shared Astur-Leonese phonological core includes: full betacism (vв†’b), Leonese e-diphthongization (eв†’ie), o-diphthongization (oв†’uo), e-syncope before s+C, imperfect -aba/-ГЎban, chв†’Кѓ, tchв†’tКѓ, xв†’Кѓ, Г§/zв†’Оё, c+e/iв†’Оё, g+e/iв†’x, positional rhotics (trill word-initial/post-C, flap elsewhere). DIVERGENCES FROM RIONORESE (morphological/lexical вЂ” not encodable in graphemeв†’IPA system, documented here for reference): (1) MASCULINE SINGULAR ARTICLE: Guadramilese uses Portuguese 'o' (not Rionorese/Leonese 'al'). Feminine 'la', masculine plural 'los', feminine plural 'las' remain Leonese вЂ” a frequency-driven partial paradigm replacement audio-confirmed in Paper III В§5.1. Code-switching analysis rules out transcription artefact: INF2, the most dialectal speaker, consistently produces 'o noibo', 'o cura', 'o almuorГ§o' (Portuguese article + maximally dialectal noun). This is the highest-confidence Guadramilese isogloss. (2) 3PL IMPERFECT 3RD CONJUGATION: '-ien' (dezien, fazien, salГ­en) vs. Rionorese '-ian'. 3 consistent attestations, no counter-examples. Aligns with central Asturian; Rionorese aligns with the western pattern. (3) IMPERFECT OF 'ir': 'diba/diban' (from Leonese infinitive stem 'dir-', regularized: dirв†’diba as tenerв†’tenia). 6+ attestations, no counter-examples. Not documented in Rionorese; represents a morphological innovation (paradigm leveling of the suppletive ir/iba pair). (4) 1SG PRONOUN: 'you' [jow] vs. Rionorese 'ieu/iou' [jew]. Both Leonese forms (cf. Asturian 'yo', Mirandese 'you'). (5) PRODUCTIVE e-SYNCOPE: es+Cв†’s+C extends productively beyond 'estarв†’star' to general position (smola < esmola). Rionorese treats only 'estar' as a lexical exception. (6) con-в†’cum- PREFIX RAISING: /o/в†’/u/ in unstressed prefix before voiced C (cumbidaba < convidava, cumbersar < conversar). 2 attestations; not confirmed in Rionorese. (7) SIBILANT REALIZATION (low-confidence, tentative): transcript 'diГ§' may indicate Guadramilese retains dental /Оё/ or /s/ where Rionorese shifted to palatal /Кѓ/ in the 'dizer' paradigm вЂ” but this may be a transcription artefact. Requires audio confirmation. Not encoded in this spec.
